candidates.name: Gmail Shared Warmup Inbox Matcher
candidates.pitch: Matches users who want warmup partners and schedules automated open/reply simulations between each others emails. Solves a real pain for cold emailers and has clear network effects. Tip jar model work
candidates.pain: Cold email deliverability suffers when sending domains are new and unestablished with no engagement history.

candidates.name: Gmail Attachment Followup Reminder
candidates.pitch: Scans email threads, identifies mentioned but unlinked attachments, and surfaces a followup reminder badge. Integrates with Gmail UI. Simple build with high ROI for sales teams. Works well as a tip ja
candidates.pain: Sales reps forget to follow up on attachments promised in emails, losing credibility and deal momentum.

candidates.name: GitHub PR Template Auto-Filler
candidates.pitch: Analyzes the code diff and fills in standard PR template sections like what changed, why, and test steps. Ships as a browser extension that reads the diff API. Freemium tier unlocks custom template ru
candidates.pain: Developers spend time manually writing PR descriptions that follow team templates when the code diff tells the story.

monetization_hint.model: Freemium → SaaS bridge (paid tier via external Stripe)

candidates.name: Email Thread Summary Bullet Points
candidates.pitch: Uses a lightweight LLM call to extract bullet-point summaries from email thread context. Can run client-side with a small model or call a simple API. Tip jar works well here since it is a per-use valu
candidates.pain: Long email threads with dozens of replies are tedious to parse and key takeaways are buried in replies.

candidates.name: CSS Grid Gap Visual Inspector
candidates.pitch: Overlays pixel measurements on all grid gaps, track gaps, and column widths directly on the page. One-click activation from devtools. Solves a frequent frustration for frontend developers and designer
candidates.pain: Debugging CSS grid layouts requires manually calculating gaps or using browser devtools which are clunky for this specific task.

monetization_hint.model: Freemium → SaaS bridge (paid tier via external Stripe)

candidates.name: Gong Call Objection Density Heatmap
candidates.pitch: Runs on Gong replay pages, counts objection keyword density per call segment, and renders a heatmap timeline. Per-seat pricing targets sales teams with active Gong usage. One-engineer buildable by par
candidates.pain: Sales managers cannot quickly see where in a call prospects raised objections and need coaching.

candidates.name: JSON Schema Fake Data Generator
candidates.pitch: Parses JSON Schema and generates fake but realistic-looking data using Faker.js patterns. The extension injects this into request bodies or form fields with one click. Simple build, high utility for d
candidates.pain: Creating realistic fake data for testing APIs and forms from JSON schemas is tedious and error-prone.

monetization_hint.model: Stripe tip jar (Payment Links $3/$5/$10) + optional sponsor link

candidates.name: Browser Macro Conditional Executor
candidates.pitch: Visual rule builder that listens to DOM events and triggers actions like click, type, or redirect. Think Zapier for the browser tab. Addresses the no-code browser automation gap that tools like Automa
candidates.pain: Repetitive browser tasks like auto-filling forms, clicking buttons, or monitoring DOM changes require custom scripts.

monetization_hint.model: Stripe tip jar (Payment Links $3/$5/$10) + optional sponsor link

candidates.name: IndexedDB Schema Side Panel
candidates.pitch: Opens a side panel that reads and visualizes all IndexedDB stores, their keyPaths, and indexes for the current origin. Allows inline data browsing and export. Developer-facing tool with clear utility.
candidates.pain: Debugging IndexedDB data requires using browser devtools which have no visualization of the data schema or relationships.

monetization_hint.model: Stripe tip jar (Payment Links $3/$5/$10) + optional sponsor link
